question: List the customer name of every customer that has no order at all.
generated_sql:
SELECT p.patient_name
FROM patients p
LEFT JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
LEFT JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
LEFT JOIN lab_results l ON a.appointment_id = l.appointment_id
LEFT JOIN prescriptions pr ON a.appointment_id = pr.appointment_id
LEFT JOIN medications m ON pr.medication_id = m.medication_id
LEFT JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
WHERE a.appointment_id IS NULL
  AND i.invoice_id IS NULL
  AND l.lab_id IS NULL
  AND pr.prescription_id IS NULL
  AND m.medication_id IS NULL
  AND d.doctor_id IS NULL

question: Which customers never appear in orders? Show the customer name.

LEFT JOIN prescriptions pr ON a.appointment_id = pr.appointment_id
LEFT JOIN medications m ON pr.medication_id = m.medication_id
LEFT JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
WHERE i.invoice_id IS NULL
  AND l.lab_id IS NULL
  AND pr.prescription_id IS NULL
  AND m.medication_id IS NULL

  AND a.appointment_id IS NULL

SELECT p.nameFirst, p.nameLast, b.SO
FROM Batting b
JOIN People p ON b.playerID = p.playerID
WHERE b.yearID = 2015
  AND b.SO >= 250

question: In 2015, who recorded strikeouts at least 250? Include the value.
generated_sql:
SELECT p.nameFirst, p.nameLast, SUM(pt.so) AS so
FROM people p
JOIN pitching pt ON p.playerID = pt.playerID
WHERE pt.yearID = 2015
GROUP BY p.playerID
HAVING SUM(pt.so) >= 250

question: List first and last names with saves for 2008 where saves at least 40.
generated_sql:
SELECT p.nameFirst, p.nameLast
FROM People p
JOIN Pitching pi ON p.playerID = pi.playerID
WHERE pi.yearID = 2008
GROUP BY p.playerID
HAVING SUM(pi.sv) >= 40

question: Which players had saves at least 40 in 2008? Show names and the value.
generated_sql:
SELECT p.nameFirst, p.nameLast, SUM(pitching.sv) AS sv
FROM people p
JOIN pitching ON p.playerID = pitching.playerID
WHERE pitching.yearID = 2008
GROUP BY p.playerID
HAVING SUM(pitching.sv) >= 40

question: Show player names and saves for the 2008 season when it was at least 40.
generated_sql:
SELECT p.nameFirst, p.nameLast, pi.sv
FROM people p
JOIN pitching pi ON p.playerID = pi.playerID
WHERE pi.yearID = 2008 AND pi.sv >= 40

question: In 2008, who recorded saves at least 40? Include the value.
generated_sql:
SELECT p.nameFirst, p.nameLast, SUM(pitching.sv) AS total_saves
FROM people p
JOIN pitching ON p.playerID = pitching.playerID
WHERE pitching.yearID = 2008
GROUP BY p.playerID
HAVING SUM(pitching.sv) >= 40

question: Which 2010 teams did not win the World Series that year?
generated_sql:
SELECT t.teamID, t.yearID
FROM Teams t
WHERE t.yearID = 2010
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM SeriesPost sp
    WHERE sp.yearID = 2010
    AND sp.round = 'WS'
    AND sp.teamIDwinner = t.teamID
)

question: List the names of 2010 teams without a 2010 World Series title.
generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT t.name
FROM Teams t
WHERE t.yearid = 2010
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM SeriesPost sp
    WHERE sp.yearid = 2010
      AND sp.round = 'World Series'
      AND sp.teamIDwinner = t.teamID
  )

question: Show every team from 2010 that failed to win the World Series.
generated_sql:

FROM Teams t
WHERE t.yearID = 2010
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1

    WHERE sp.yearID = t.yearID
    AND sp.round = 'WS'
    AND sp.teamIDwinner = t.teamID
)

question: Which finished products never appear in product reviews? Show the name.
List finished-goods products with no product reviews records.
generated_sql:

FROM Product p
WHERE p.FinishedGoodsFlag = 1
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM ProductReview pr
    WHERE pr.ProductID = p.ProductID
)

question: Find sellable products missing from product reviews.

SELECT d.doctor_id, d.doctor_name
FROM doctors d
JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.doctor_name
HAVING SUM(i.total_amount) > (
    SELECT AVG(doctor_total)
    FROM (
        SELECT d2.doctor_id, SUM(i2.total_amount) AS doctor_total
        FROM doctors d2
        JOIN appointments a2 ON d2.doctor_id = a2.doctor_id
        JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
        GROUP BY d2.doctor_id
    )
)
warnings: selected SQL: possible join fanout aggregate inflation

question: Which patients have no appointment with status 'cancelled'? Show patient name.

FROM patients p

question: Among 2019 qualified batters, which rows have home run count above the average? Show player id and home runs.
generated_sql:
SELECT playerid, SUM(hr) AS total_hr
FROM batting
WHERE yearid = 2019
GROUP BY playerid
HAVING SUM(hr) > (
    SELECT AVG(player_hr_sum)
    FROM (
        SELECT SUM(hr) AS player_hr_sum
        FROM batting
        WHERE yearid = 2019
        GROUP BY playerid
    )
)

question: Find 2019 qualified batters entries with above-average home run count.
generated_sql:
WITH player_totals AS (
    SELECT playerid, SUM(hr) AS total_hr
    FROM batting
    WHERE yearid = 2019
    GROUP BY playerid
),
avg_hr AS (
    SELECT AVG(total_hr) AS avg_total_hr
    FROM player_totals
)
SELECT pt.playerid, 2019 AS yearid, pt.total_hr AS hr
FROM player_totals pt
CROSS JOIN avg_hr a
WHERE pt.total_hr > a.avg_total_hr

question: Show player id and home runs for 2019 qualified batters whose home run count exceeds the group average.

  FROM batting

  GROUP BY playerid
),
avg_hr AS (

  FROM player_totals
)
SELECT pt.playerid, pt.total_hr AS hr
FROM player_totals pt
CROSS JOIN avg_hr a
WHERE pt.total_hr > a.avg_val

question: In 2016, which players out-earned their own team's average salary?

  SELECT yearid, teamid, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary
  FROM salaries
  WHERE yearid = 2016
  GROUP BY yearid, teamid
)
SELECT s.playerid, s.salary
FROM salaries s
JOIN team_avg ta ON s.yearid = ta.yearid AND s.teamid = ta.teamid
WHERE s.yearid = 2016
  AND s.salary > ta.avg_salary

question: Find 2005 salaries above the same-team average.
generated_sql:
WITH team_avg AS (
  SELECT yearid, teamid, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary
  FROM salaries
  GROUP BY yearid, teamid
),
overall_avg AS (
  SELECT AVG(avg_salary) AS global_avg
  FROM team_avg
)
SELECT s.yearid, s.teamid, s.playerid, s.salary
FROM salaries s
JOIN team_avg t ON s.yearid = t.yearid AND s.teamid = t.teamid
CROSS JOIN overall_avg o
WHERE s.yearid = 2005
  AND s.salary > t.avg_salary
  AND t.avg_salary > o.global_avg

question: Find the 2 lab tests with the greatest average value.
generated_sql:
WITH per_test_avg AS (
  SELECT test_name, AVG(test_value) AS avg_val
  FROM lab_results
  GROUP BY test_name
),
global_avg AS (
  SELECT AVG(avg_val) AS threshold
  FROM per_test_avg
)
SELECT t.test_name
FROM per_test_avg t
CROSS JOIN global_avg g
WHERE t.avg_val > g.threshold
ORDER BY t.avg_val DESC
LIMIT 2

generated_sql:

    SELECT m.medication_id, m.medication_name, COUNT(p.prescription_id) AS cnt

    GROUP BY m.medication_id, m.medication_name

question: List the 10 highest-strikeout count 2015 starters.
generated_sql:
WITH starters_2015 AS (
    SELECT playerID
    FROM appearances
    WHERE yearID = 2015 AND gs > 0
),
batting_agg AS (
    SELECT playerID, SUM(so) AS total_so
    FROM batting
    WHERE yearID = 2015
    GROUP BY playerID
)
SELECT p.nameFirst, p.nameLast, ba.total_so
FROM batting_agg ba
JOIN starters_2015 s ON ba.playerID = s.playerID
JOIN people p ON ba.playerID = p.playerID
ORDER BY ba.total_so DESC
LIMIT 10

question: For each customer, how many distinct product category purchaseds are there?
generated_sql:
SELECT p.customer_id, COUNT(DISTINCT pr.category) AS distinct_categories
FROM customers p
JOIN orders o ON p.customer_id = o.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
JOIN products pr ON oi.product_id = pr.product_id
GROUP BY p.customer_id

question: Count unique product category purchaseds per customer.
generated_sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, COUNT(DISTINCT m.medication_class) AS unique_categories
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
JOIN lab_results l ON a.appointment_id = l.appointment_id
JOIN prescriptions pr ON a.appointment_id = pr.appointment_id
JOIN medications m ON pr.medication_id = m.medication_id
JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
GROUP BY p.patient_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT m.medication_class) > 0

JOIN products pr ON oi.product_id = pr.product_id

question: Per school, how many distinct players since 1990?
generated_sql:
WITH latest_per_player AS (
    SELECT cp.playerid, cp.schoolid, cp.yearid,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cp.playerid ORDER BY cp.yearid DESC) AS rn
    FROM collegeplaying cp
)
SELECT s.name_full, COUNT(DISTINCT lp.playerid) AS distinct_players
FROM latest_per_player lp
JOIN schools s ON lp.schoolid = s.schoolid
WHERE lp.rn = 1 AND lp.yearid >= 1990
GROUP BY s.name_full

question: Compute insurance paid as a percent of total billed for each insurance provider.
generated_sql:
WITH provider_totals AS (
  SELECT 
    p.insurance_provider,
    SUM(i.insurance_paid) AS total_insurance_paid,
    SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_billed
  FROM appointments a
  JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
  JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id
  GROUP BY p.insurance_provider
),
global_averages AS (
  SELECT 
    AVG(total_insurance_paid) AS avg_paid,
    AVG(total_billed) AS avg_billed
  FROM provider_totals
)
SELECT 
  pt.insurance_provider
FROM provider_totals pt
CROSS JOIN global_averages ga
WHERE pt.total_insurance_paid > ga.avg_paid
  AND pt.total_billed > ga.avg_billed

question: Show each insurance provider with its insurance paid-to-total billed percentage.
generated_sql:
WITH provider_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        p.insurance_provider,
        SUM(i.insurance_paid) AS total_insurance_paid,
        SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_billed
    FROM invoices i
    JOIN appointments a ON i.appointment_id = a.appointment_id
    JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id
    GROUP BY p.insurance_provider
),
provider_percentages AS (
    SELECT 
        insurance_provider,
        (total_insurance_paid * 100.0 / total_billed) AS percentage
    FROM provider_stats
)
SELECT 
    insurance_provider,
    percentage
FROM provider_percentages
WHERE percentage > (SELECT AVG(percentage) FROM provider_percentages)

question: Per vendor, compute the average PO line value per order.
generated_sql:
SELECT v.Name
FROM Vendor v
JOIN PurchaseOrderHeader poh ON v.BusinessEntityID = poh.VendorID
JOIN PurchaseOrderDetail pod ON poh.PurchaseOrderID = pod.PurchaseOrderID
GROUP BY v.BusinessEntityID, v.Name
HAVING AVG(pod.LineTotal) > 0

question: Show each vendor's average PO line value per order.
generated_sql:
SELECT v.Name
FROM Vendor v
JOIN PurchaseOrderHeader poh ON v.BusinessEntityID = poh.VendorID
JOIN PurchaseOrderDetail pod ON poh.PurchaseOrderID = pod.PurchaseOrderID
GROUP BY v.BusinessEntityID, v.Name
HAVING AVG(pod.LineTotal) = (
    SELECT MAX(avg_val)
    FROM (
        SELECT AVG(pod2.LineTotal) as avg_val
        FROM Vendor v2
        JOIN PurchaseOrderHeader poh2 ON v2.BusinessEntityID = poh2.VendorID
        JOIN PurchaseOrderDetail pod2 ON poh2.PurchaseOrderID = pod2.PurchaseOrderID
        GROUP BY v2.BusinessEntityID
    )
)

question: For every vendor, report the average PO line value per order.

JOIN PurchaseOrderDetail pod ON poh.PurchaseOrderID = pod.PurchaseOrderID
GROUP BY v.BusinessEntityID, v.Name

question: Group by vendor and calculate the average PO line value per order.
generated_sql:
SELECT v.Name
FROM Vendor v
JOIN PurchaseOrderHeader poh ON v.BusinessEntityID = poh.VendorID
JOIN PurchaseOrderDetail pod ON poh.PurchaseOrderID = pod.PurchaseOrderID
GROUP BY v.BusinessEntityID, v.Name
HAVING AVG(pod.LineTotal) = (
    SELECT AVG(pod2.LineTotal)
    FROM PurchaseOrderDetail pod2
    JOIN PurchaseOrderHeader poh2 ON pod2.PurchaseOrderID = poh2.PurchaseOrderID
    WHERE poh2.VendorID = v.BusinessEntityID
)

question: Per ship method, compute the average freight percentage.

SELECT sm.Name
FROM SalesOrderHeader soh
JOIN ShipMethod sm ON soh.ShipMethodID = sm.ShipMethodID
GROUP BY sm.ShipMethodID, sm.Name
HAVING AVG(soh.Freight * 1.0 / soh.TotalDue) = (
    SELECT MAX(avg_freight_pct)
    FROM (
        SELECT AVG(Freight * 1.0 / TotalDue) AS avg_freight_pct
        FROM SalesOrderHeader
        WHERE TotalDue > 0
        GROUP BY ShipMethodID
    )
)

GROUP BY sm.ShipMethodID, sm.Name

question: Report manufactured products over all products (as %) per product line.
generated_sql:
SELECT productline, productid FROM (
  SELECT 
    p.ProductLine AS productline, 
    p.ProductID AS productid,
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN p.MakeFlag = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS percentage
  FROM Product p
  GROUP BY p.ProductLine
) sub
WHERE percentage > 0
